etymology.description: Depicts 又(又) (yòu, "hand") holding a stylized flag or stick
etymology.interpretation: The character 吏(lì, "official") represents a hand holding a flag or stick, which is indicative of an official or ambassador role. It shares historical roots with 史(史) (shǐ, "history"), which originally depicted a similar figure. In 吏(lì), the stylized depiction of a tool in a hand signifies the function of an official or representative.

examples.chinese: 古代的官吏(古代的官吏)负责管理地方事务(負責管理地方事務)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔdài de guānlì fùzé guǎnlǐ dìfāng shìwù.
examples.english: Officials in ancient times were responsible for managing local affairs.

examples.chinese: 这位吏员(這位吏員)在衙门工作(在衙門工作)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè wèi lì yuán zài yámén gōngzuò.
examples.english: This official works in the government office.

examples.chinese: 官吏的职责(官吏的職責)是为百姓服务(是為百姓服務)。
examples.pinyin: Guānlì de zhízé shì wèi bǎixìng fúwù.
examples.english: The duty of officials is to serve the people.
